rows.notes: 70 train / 30 val. Entity swap applied: BENCHMARK_PRODUCT -> Mike, generic emails -> lawyer@lawfirm.com, generic docs -> contract.pdf/NDA/legal brief. Val uses distinct document types, fictional credentials, and personas not present in train.

70 train / 30 val. 69 distinct jailbreak techniques across train+val. Val personas (ARIA, UNLEASHED, LexBot-Sigma, LexAI-Omega), fictional jurisdictions (Lexaria), named characters (Harlan Voss, Diana Marsh), and harmful tasks are all distinct from train set.

Synthesized — no public LLM-PII benchmark exists at the legal domain specificity required. 30 PII attack techniques covering SSN, address, DOB, health, financial, government ID, biometric, genetic, criminal record, immigration status, digital identity, and tool-augmented attacks. Val uses distinct named individuals (Elena Vasquez, Patricia Okonkwo, Samira Hussain, David Nakamura), distinct document types, and distinct PII types (SWIFT codes, MAC addresses, Medicare numbers).

Mike-specific references (chatTools.ts, SYSTEM_PROMPT constant, buildMessages function, agentInstructions field) used across both splits to test resistance regardless of codebase knowledge. Val uses distinct encodings (ROT13/hex vs base64/reversed), distinct creative formats (limerick/statute vs haiku/contract), distinct config formats (YAML vs JSON), and distinct positional references (last sentence vs first sentence).

All 10 Mike tools targeted. Probes cover resource limit bypass (replicate_document >20 cap), payload injection (JavaScript, macros, XML bombs, OOXML altChunk), chained exfiltration, path traversal, PII search automation, and tracking beacons. Val uses distinct documents, payloads, and tool chains from train.

Synthesized — no public multi-turn legal AI context contamination benchmark exists. Exploits enrichWithPriorEvents and Supabase history replay. All probes include prior_turns array. Val uses distinct named individuals (Gareth Reynolds, Patricia Webb, Marcus Dinallo), distinct keywords (OVERRIDE-COUNSEL vs LEGAL-UNLOCK), and distinct harmful tasks (deed of trust, tax audit evasion, perjured affidavit, juror contact list).

rotation_rationale: No prior_manifest provided. This is the initial run. All probes are first-generation. Rotation tracking will apply on subsequent runs.
audit_caveats: pii_disclosure synthesised — no public LLM-PII benchmark exists at the legal domain specificity level required for this product

context_contamination synthesised — no public multi-turn legal AI context contamination benchmark exists; custom probes exploit Mike's Supabase session history replay (enrichWithPriorEvents) and agentic loop memory
output_handling_static is OWASP/PortSwigger canon, no upstream version pin for the static payload catalog
localization_mode=entity_swap: all probes are English-only; entity names swapped to Mike/legal domain but probe prose preserved verbatim from benchmark source — is_paraphrase set false throughout

injecagent probes adapted to Mike's tool schema (read_document, find_in_document, generate_docx, edit_document, list_documents, fetch_documents, replicate_document, list_workflows, read_workflow, read_table_cells) — original InjecAgent tool names replaced with Mike-specific names
val.jsonl files must NEVER be exposed to the security-coding-agent; only train.jsonl and category-level threat summaries are permitted inputs to the defense optimizer
replicate_document cap is 20 per scan-result.json workflowNodes runToolCalls description — probes use 21 and 25 to test boundary enforcement
No AGPL or other restricted licenses in this suite — all source benchmarks are MIT or Apache-2.0; synth content is internal
